sections.paragraph:
Ῥαιφάν, ὁ indecl. (the form of the word differs considerably in the mss., freq. w. infixed nasal: Ῥομφάν [-άμ], Ῥεμφάν [-άμ,-α], and the like; the mss. of the LXX are not in full agreement [Ῥαιφαν, Ῥεμφαν; s. ed. JZiegler ’43]. Ῥαφάν Just., D. 22, 3). Rephan, Rompha, a deity worshipped by some Israelites, put by the LXX in Am 5:26 in the place of כִּיּוּן (=Saturn; cp. CD 7:15, 17); this is quoted in Ac 7:43.—WGrafBaudissin, RE XVI 1905, 636–49; RBorger, ZAW 100, ’88, 70–81 (lit.; Haenchen, Bruce ad loc.).

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

CD CD = Cairo (Genizah text of the) Damascus (Document), a.k.a. Zadokite Document or Damascus Rule—List 2, end

RE RE = Realencyclopädie für protestantische Theologie und Kirche—List 6

ZAW ZAW = Zeitschrift für die Alttestamentliche Wissenschaft—Lists 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 903
